payload.description: Full length of 34th Street in Manhattan from the East River to the Hudson River, proposed transitway corridor for BRT

evidence_refs.source_quote: 34 th Street Transitway

A map of the 34th Street Transitway in New York City. The transitway is shown as a red and white striped line running diagonally from the top left to the bottom right. It passes through several streets and stations. The map includes a legend in the bottom left corner with a north arrow, a scale bar (0 to 0.25 miles), and symbols for BRT Stations (blue hexagons), Commuter/Express Bus Stops (blue 'X' marks), and the Proposed Transitway (red and white striped line). The map also shows various bus lines (A, C, E, 1, 2, 3, 6, B, D, F, M, N, Q, R) and street names (Twelfth Avenue, Eleventh Avenue, Tenth Avenue, Ninth Avenue, Eighth Avenue, Seventh Avenue, Sixth Avenue, Madison, Park Avenue, Third Avenue, Second Avenue, First Avenue, E 34th Street Ferry Terminal). The map is set against a background of a street grid and some green spaces.
Enhanced bus lanes for full length of 34 th Street
